title: Remove docker-compose db service foot-gun (split into bootstrap.yml)

steps.description: Rename docker-compose.yml -> docker-compose.bootstrap.yml; add top-level `name: iw-ai-core`; stub or delete default compose file; update ai-core.sh cmd_db start/stop/logs to use -f flag; update Makefile db-up target.
Code review: S01 (correct -f usage, env-var pass-through preserved, ai-core.sh guard intact, no hardcoded paths/creds, no regression in cmd_db stop/logs paths).

Write docs/IW_AI_Core_DB_Setup.md as the single source of truth for DB setup (production raw docker + bootstrap compose paths). Update top-level README.md, top-level CLAUDE.md, docs/README.md, docs/IW_AI_Core_Tech_Stack.md, docs/implementation/01_foundation/02_config_and_db.md to explain WHY the split exists (2026-04-22 incident) and point to the new doc. Grep for stray `docker compose up.*db` references anywhere and fix.
Code review: S03 (every affected doc updated, WHY paragraph present, pointer to new doc, no stale references, new doc has both production and bootstrap paths clearly labeled).

Shell-level smoke tests: docker compose config from root has no db service; docker compose -f docker-compose.bootstrap.yml config has db; volume name stable across cwd (name: iw-ai-core); ai-core.sh db start no-ops when DB already up.
Code review: S05 (tests exercise the actual foot-gun scenario, tests fail against pre-change code, no hardcoded host paths, portable across dev machines).

Final cross-layer review: no stray docker compose up -d db references anywhere in repo; ai-core.sh wiring correct; CR-00014 identity check still green; docs all agree; incident rationale explained in every dev-facing doc.
